_comment: Recall-librarian fail-closed permission posture (piece C). Deploys to ~/librarian/.claude/settings.json. THE CONFINEMENT (cross-family review r1): fail-closed by denying the FULL current canonical NON-Bash tool surface by name (verified vs Claude Code 2.1.x tools-reference). Bash is the ONE tool left un-denied — because a settings `deny` OVERRIDES a hook `allow` (deny-first precedence), so `deny:["*"]` would kill Bash and the recall-gate could never allow a valid `mxr ask`. So: deny every non-Bash tool here; the PreToolUse recall-gate is the SOLE allow-er of Bash and only allows an exact `mxr ask --scope research|fitness "<safe question>"` (fail-closed on every other path). RESIDUAL: this list must be extended when Claude Code adds a NEW no-prompt tool (there is no deny-all-except-Bash primitive). MCP: `disableClaudeAiConnectors:true` blocks the claude.ai connectors; the launch ALSO sets CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_MCP=1 (README) to load zero MCP servers — `mcp__*` here is belt. Edit the hook path if the checkout is not /Users/stevenfernandez/code/active/myndaix-runtime.
